issue_title: Postgres Encoding
issue_body:
Trying to do a fresh setup of Kinto using the Python packages with Postgres. I am running into an issue where running ``kinto migrate`` is giving me the error:

```sh
/home/ubuntu/workspace/kinto/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cliquet/storage/postgresql/client.py:86: UserWarning: Reuse existing PostgreSQL connection. Parameters permission_* will be ignored.
  warnings.warn(msg)
/home/ubuntu/workspace/kinto/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cliquet/storage/postgresql/client.py:86: UserWarning: Reuse existing PostgreSQL connection. Parameters cache_* will be ignored.
  warnings.warn(msg)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/ubuntu/workspace/kinto/bin/kinto", line 11, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "/home/ubuntu/workspace/kinto/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/kinto/__main__.py", line 79, in main
    cliquet.init_schema(env)
  File "/home/ubuntu/workspace/kinto/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cliquet/scripts/cliquet.py", line 31, in init_schema
    getattr(registry, backend).initialize_schema()
  File "/home/ubuntu/workspace/kinto/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cliquet/storage/postgresql/__init__.py", line 93, in initialize_schema
    self._check_database_encoding()
  File "/home/ubuntu/workspace/kinto/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cliquet/storage/postgresql/__init__.py", line 144, in _check_database_encoding
    raise AssertionError('Unexpected database encoding %s' % encoding)
AssertionError: Unexpected database encoding sql_ascii
```
I have been able to reproduce this multiple times. I am running on Ubuntu Trusty (14.04 LTS) on Cloud9 (c9.io), with Postgres 9.5.1. The default database encoding (as shown below) is SQL_ASCII.

![psql](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6036093/13018957/70255512-d181-11e5-934a-7ce2bcf22140.png)

I am not sure why my default encoding is SQL_ASCII instead of UTF-8, but since the Postgres docs say you can set the encoding for a new database when creating it, I don't think that Kinto should fail if the default encoding is not what is expected.

### Issue description

Stopping and immediately starting a pipeline again leads to artifacts in color (but not depth) image:

![corrupted](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1241736/36500912-e01b58ce-1745-11e8-95e9-02fcb683ad46.png)

It looks like odd and even columns are swapped. Also if you pay attention, the first column is swapped with the last.

issue_title: Small possible divergence with original DQN paper
issue_body:
Hi,

I noticed that there might be a slight difference between this implementation of the network and the original one by DeepMind. Maybe this is a known fact, but I didn't see it mentionned anywhere, and as this implementation seems to try to be as close as possible to the original one, I thought it'd be worth it to point it out.

It boils down to the fact that this implementation uses the default padding from TensorFlow, which is 'VALID', whereas DeepMind didn't document any padding on their convolutional layers.
If we refer to the Torch implementation they released, we can conclude that they used the default padding of Torch (which is 0 in the SpatialConvolution module), except for the first layer, where they used padding=1

After the convolutions, the image sizes are quite different: 7x7 for (py)Torch and 11x11 for TensorFlow (40% difference). As such, the input size of the first linear layer diverge (3136 vs 7744)

I'm not sure that makes a huge difference (be it positive or negative) in the outcome, but experience has proved that devil's in the details when it comes to deep architectures.

What do you guys think ?

issue_title: Confused about Her+DDPG policy-loss
issue_body:
The policy-loss in the her+ddpg implementation is defined as following:
```
self.pi_loss_tf = -tf.reduce_mean(self.main.Q_pi_tf)
self.pi_loss_tf += self.action_l2 * tf.reduce_mean(tf.square(self.main.pi_tf / self.max_u))
```
This can be found here:
https://github.com/openai/baselines/blob/f2729693253c0ef4d4086231d36e0a4307ec1cb3/baselines/her/ddpg.py#L274

I understand why we are using the first part:
`self.pi_loss_tf = -tf.reduce_mean(self.main.Q_pi_tf)`
However, I do not understand the purpose of the second part (I call it from now on *mean_sqr_action*):
`self.pi_loss_tf += self.action_l2 * tf.reduce_mean(tf.square(self.main.pi_tf / self.max_u))`

The second part is never mentioned in the paper as far as I know and also isn't used in your vanilla implementation of your baseline-ddpg. Additionally, in my two experiments it improved the learning significantly when removing the mean_sqr_action from the loss function.

The first experiment was in the environment *FetchReach-v1* with the default settings. In this case it turns out that the mean_sqr_action-implementation needs 5 epochs to reach a success rate of 1 whereas the modified version needs only 3 epochs.

In the more complex environment *HandReach-v0* the mean_sqr_action-version needed 20 epochs to reach an accuracy of 0.4 whereas the modified-version could achieve an accuracy of 0.5 already after 11 epochs and after 20 epochs an accuracy of 0.55.

        initial = tf.constant(0.1, shape=shape)

    def variable_summaries(var):

        with tf.name_scope('summaries'):

            with tf.name_scope('Wx_plus_b'):
                preactivate = tf.matmul(input_tensor, weights) + biases
                tf.summary.histogram('pre_activations', preactivate)
            activations = act(preactivate, name='activation')
            tf.summary.histogram('activations', activations)
            return activations

    hidden1 = nn_layer(x, 784, 500, 'layer1')

    with tf.name_scope('dropout'):
        keep_prob = tf.placeholder(tf.float32)
        tf.summary.scalar('dropout_keep_probability', keep_prob)
        dropped = tf.nn.dropout(hidden1, keep_prob)

    # Do not apply softmax activation yet, see below.
    y = nn_layer(dropped, 500, 10, 'layer2', act=tf.identity)

    with tf.name_scope('cross_entropy'):
        diff = tf.nn.softmax_cross_entropy_with_logits(labels=y_, logits=y)
        with tf.name_scope('total'):
            cross_entropy = tf.reduce_mean(diff)
    tf.summary.scalar('cross_entropy', cross_entropy)

    with tf.name_scope('train'):
        train_step = tf.train.AdamOptimizer(FLAGS.learning_rate).minimize(
            cross_entropy)

    with tf.name_scope('accuracy'):
        with tf.name_scope('correct_prediction'):
            correct_prediction = tf.equal(tf.argmax(y, 1), tf.argmax(y_, 1))
        with tf.name_scope('accuracy'):
            accuracy = tf.reduce_mean(tf.cast(correct_prediction, tf.float32))
    tf.summary.scalar('accuracy', accuracy)

    # Merge all the summaries and write them out to
    # /tmp/tensorflow/mnist/logs/mnist_with_summaries (by default)
    merged = tf.summary.merge_all()
    train_writer = tf.summary.FileWriter(FLAGS.log_dir + '/train', sess.graph)
    test_writer = tf.summary.FileWriter(FLAGS.log_dir + '/test')
    tf.global_variables_initializer().run()

    def feed_dict(train):
        """Make a TensorFlow feed_dict: maps data onto Tensor placeholders."""
        if train or FLAGS.fake_data:
            xs, ys = mnist.train.next_batch(100, fake_data=FLAGS.fake_data)
            k = FLAGS.dropout
        else:
            xs, ys = mnist.test.images, mnist.test.labels
            k = 1.0
        return {x: xs, y_: ys, keep_prob: k}

    # test function to monitor variables!!!!
    def test_func():
        loss = sess.run(valid_loss, feed_dict={'val_loss:0': 2.0})
        print(loss)

    for i in range(FLAGS.max_steps):
        if i % 10 == 0:  # Record summaries and test-set accuracy
            summary, acc = sess.run([merged, accuracy], feed_dict=feed_dict(False))
            test_writer.add_summary(summary, i)
            print('Accuracy at step %s: %s' % (i, acc))
        else:  # Record train set summaries, and train
            if i % 100 == 99:  # Record execution stats
                run_options = tf.RunOptions(trace_level=tf.RunOptions.FULL_TRACE)
                run_metadata = tf.RunMetadata()
                summary, _ = sess.run([merged, train_step],
                                      feed_dict=feed_dict(True),
                                      options=run_options,
                                      run_metadata=run_metadata)
                train_writer.add_run_metadata(run_metadata, 'step%03d' % i)
                train_writer.add_summary(summary, i)
                print('Adding run metadata for', i)
            else:  # Record a summary
                summary, _ = sess.run([merged, train_step], feed_dict=feed_dict(True))
                train_writer.add_summary(summary, i)
                test_func()

    train_writer.close()
    test_writer.close()


def main(_):
    if tf.gfile.Exists(FLAGS.log_dir):
        tf.gfile.DeleteRecursively(FLAGS.log_dir)
    tf.gfile.MakeDirs(FLAGS.log_dir)
    train()

issue_title: Dealing with different versions of ARM embedded gcc for examples
issue_body:
Different examples have different required versions of ARM embedded gcc. For example, most work fine with 4.9.x but both the cc2650 and da15000 recommend 5.4.x. The standard package manager per platform (brew, apt-get, etc.) don't have a standard way to install multiple versions of the ARM gcc, making it difficult to switch between platforms. Although that might not be typical among most users, is there a best-practice or mod we can add to the convenience make files to point to different  build versions? Like a flag to override $(CC)? Use the specific named version, ie arm-none-eabi-gcc-4.9.3? 

Might also be handy for debian users as the name of the executable [changed](https://launchpad.net/gcc-arm-embedded/+announcement/13824).

issue_title: Requesting new Network Data if Child Update Response contains new version
issue_body:
Right now when a child handles Child Update Response message it calls `HandleLeaderData()`. Child Update Response does not contain Active or Pending Timestamps and because of that `HandleLeaderData()` does not send Data Request packet even if Network Data version in the message is greater than local value. It seems that Data Request should be sent in this case.

It is not a big problem because there is fallback procedure to check Network Data version in Advertisements transmitted by parent and this procedure works as expected.

issue_title: cli: CTRL-C shouldn't exit on embedded platforms
issue_body:
This line of code is dangerous when compiled for an embedded platform:
https://github.com/openthread/openthread/blob/master/src/cli/cli_serial.cpp#L106

Accidentally pressing CTRL-C in your CLI terminal will halt execution and require a reset.  
Perhaps it would be okay if a proper watchdog is implemented on the platform.

issue_title: Strategy for common compilers
issue_body:
While we're targeting an OSS toolchain, folks use a bunch of different compilers @ work. IAR seems to be a common request and one that doesn't work out of the box. See [this SO](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/37767178/openthread-compiling-errors-relating-to-prefixtlv) question as an example.

It's not feasible to target every toolchain, but we should at least be able to come up with a strategy to help people use their preferred (or required) toolchains.

issue_title: Router floods network with Child Update Requests after reset
issue_body:
We see a suspicious behavior of a router/leader after reset. If the router had any children then, after reset, it will send a child Update Request to each of them. The issue is that it keeps doing so every second, for about 240 seconds (I guess until all children are removed from the child table). Is that intended?

Steps to reproduce:
- start a device and wait until it promotes to a leader
- start another device and wait until it attaches as a child
- switch off the second device
- reset the first device and observe the issue

issue_title: RGB values are defined as floats instead of integers.
issue_body:
RGB values are intended to be integers and the HTTP/JSON response from the [Vision API contains the correct values](https://cloud.google.com/vision/docs/detecting-properties).

The gRPC/protobuf version however has the RGB values set to floats and documented as [0, 1] values.
See:
https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/c329e5b1723111925ac81458955eaeccdf6ebb4d/google/type/color.proto#L142-L164

issue_title: Consolidate functionality of editing, saving, and showing the history of commands entered
issue_body:
The **do_history()** command allows you to search and slice history before displaying it. The **do_save()** command doesn't, but it will save history to a file. The **do_edit()** command lets you edit one history item and then run it. We should consolidate all the history functionality into a single command.

- [x] Add a **-o/--output-file** argument to **do_history()** which writes the selected history item(s) to the given file
- [x] Remove the **do_save()** method and it's associated tests
- [x] Add a **-r/--run** argument to **do_history()** to run the selected command(s)
- [x] Remove the **do_run()** method and it's associated tests
- [x] Add a **-e/--edit** argument to **do_history()** to let you edit the selected history item(s) in an editor. Run the contents of the edited file when the editor exits.
- [x] Modify **do_edit()** to not edit commands from history, just to edit files
- [x] Remove **_last_matching()** method
- [x] Remove **autorun_on_edit** attribute and setting

issue_title: Mailjet webhook events fail when not grouped
issue_body:
Currently (django-anymail v2.2) produces a TypeError when an event type is not configured to be grouped in Mailjet's event trigger settings. This is because the Mailjet webhook handling assumes the body to always be a JSON list containing all events although this is not the case when an event type is not marked to "Group events", which will send each event individually as a JSON dict.
This leads to ```TypeError: string indices must be integers``` in webhooks/mailjet.py:57 since ```esp_event``` is now a string (key of the event dict).

I guess the reason it is currently implemented like this is that it seems like the official documentation doesn't actually consider this and just says ```All the events will be delivered to your webhook in a JSON array of event objects.``` (https://dev.mailjet.com/guides/#endpoint-url)

It is the same issue as https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/issues/642 but I'd like to provide a non-obscure use case.

In the interface I'm writing I'd like to provide a constructor from a Python list. The elements of the list can be double, string and None (ideally). The constructor being exposed is this one: 
`A::A(const vector<B>& l)`
I expose it using
```
py::class_<A> m(module, "A");
m.def(py::init<vector<B>>&>())
```
I'd like to call this using:
```
A([None, 1.0, "string"])
```
I registered implicit conversion from `double` and `string` to `B` and it flies, that is I can create object `A` using `A([1.0, 'string'])`. This works because `B` has implicit conversions:
`B.def(py::init<double>()).def(py::init<std::string>()).def(py::init<>());`
and a list of `py::implicitly_convertible<double, B>()...`. 

Question: How to I add implicit conversion from Python None to use B() when in a list?

issue_body:
I would like to implement a different local storage format, namely the one that [tudu](https://code.meskio.net/tudu/) uses. I like the interface of tudu more than the interface of todoman, and I would like to use vdirsyncer to sync a remote server with its storage.

I can basically do one of two things:
* Add a storage type to vdirsyncer for the XML-based format tudu uses.
* Add support for vdir-type storage to tudu.

Of these, I prefer the first because it is probably much simpler to implement, as vdirsyncer already has support for several local storage types.

* Do you have some pointers on where to start?

issue_body:
I'm on Ubuntu 16.04.1 with the deb letsencrypt 0.4.1-1. I have a section in my apache `mydomain.com-ssl.conf` as
```
<LocationMatch ^/ec/(?<userid>foo|bar)>
  Require user %{env:MATCH_USERID}
</LocationMatch>
```
This is perfectly [valid](https://httpd.apache.org/docs/2.4/mod/core.html#locationmatch) (as of apache 2.4.8) and working just fine, but letsencrypt chokes with
```

The error was: PluginError(('There has been an error in parsing the file (%s): %s', u'/etc/apache2/sites-enabled/mydomain.com-ssl.conf', u'Syntax error'),)

Commenting out the section makes letsencrypt happy.

issue_title: Blanket X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN in Apache shared config
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version): Debian 9.3 Stretch ## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc): apt, version 0.10.2 is installed ## Issue: On my system, Certbot installed an Apache configuration file in `/etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-apache.conf` that is included in all the automatically generated sites config. It features the following line: `Header always set X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN`. It sort of makes sense to harden the web server on top of using HTTPS. ## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because: It breaks WordPress OEmbed feature. OEmbed allows websites to embed a HTML snippet to display a nice render of a blog post. However, the WordPress snippet includes an iframe to an internal embed URL that can't be displayed on third-party websites if X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN is set for all requests. Example WordPress embed snippet: ```html
Reporters on icebergs while DC tells Minnesota to ‘stuff’ our weather expertise
``` However, [the 0.10.x version of the options-ssl-apache.conf](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/0.10.x/certbot-apache/certbot_apache/options-ssl-apache.conf) doesn't show this line, so I was wondering who could have made the change and how to prevent it. I was able to comment out this line on my own server, but I can't do it on `blogs.mprnews.org` for example, which breaks their embeds without their knowledge.

issue_title: Clarify documentation of configuration file
issue_body:
https://certbot.eff.org/docs/using.html#configuration-file says::

```
# All flags used by the client can be configured here. Run Certbot with
# "--help" to learn more about the available options.
```

This is ambiguous and begs the question of whether there are configuration options that are not flags to the client. This should be clarified, either by discussing which options are configuration file only, or changing the above to something like:

```
# The possible configuration options are exactly the same as the flags used by the client.
# Run Certbot with "--help" to learn more about the available options.
```

issue_title: echo_node has to depend on get_payment_events_for_token
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

After the changes I did in #2038 where the payment endpoint was added, the echo_node has to be changed, at the moment it uses [get_channel_events]( https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/utils/echo_node.py#L99 ) which returns more then just the payment events. It should use the [get_payment_events_for_token](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/api/python.py#L673). 

This brings  us to another problem, we should adapt the logic of the echo_node... previously the get_channel_events was returning a dictionary and now the get_payment_events_for_token returns a tuple. 
 
### Proposed solutions

Change the logic of the echo_node to ensure it works with the output provided by get_payment_events.

issue_title: define connection manager behavior
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Connection manager (CM) is used to automatically join the token network using a certain amount of funds. It will automatically create payment channels with random peers and will distribute the funds equally across the channels.

There're several problems with the implementation
- the peers to create the channels with are chosen randomly, regardless of their state. Online nodes should be preferred instead.
- current implementation could use some refactoring in regard to parallel `connect()` calls. Now it just serializes parallel operations with a lock, which can sometime lead to confusing results.
- the REST api allows multiple calls to `connect()` method, even when the network channel join/deposit cycle is in progress. In such case it would be appropriate to return E409 if the network join is in progress. A GET request to `/api/1/connections/<token_address>` should return status of connection manager as well

To play devils advocate, I think that connection manager is an ideal candidate to remove from Raiden core - anything it does could be done in a couple of REST calls, either sent by a cli tool or a webUI.

issue_title: Handle old balance proofs used for settlement by the channel partner
issue_body:
Raiden client keeps the last balance proof data in the channel state.
If the partner uses an older balance proof, there is currently no implemented way to find that data.

As @hackaugusto pointed out, we can either iterate through all the WAL data (bytes encoded) and decode it and check if it is what we are looking for, or change the current storage layout and add an additional table.

The same issue for pending locks from old balance proofs. These are not currently stored in the WAL at all.

The same issue might apply for the participant data, in case the channel is closed with an old balance proof, provided by a 3rd party.

issue_title: ERC20 Recover tokens transferred with transfer instead of transferFrom
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Raiden netting channel contract is a special contract that is designed to work with token deposits.

It requires users to send tokens to this contract using [deposit](https://github.com/EthereumCommonwealth/raiden/blob/master/raiden/smart_contracts/NettingChannelLibrary.sol#L67) function which will call the `transferFrom` function of the desired ERC-20 token.

ERC-20 token standard suffers critical security problem: it has two ways to transfer funds (1) [transfer](https://github.com/EthereumCommonwealth/raiden/blob/SafeRaiden/raiden/smart_contracts/Token.sol#L16) function and (2) [approve + transferFrom](https://github.com/EthereumCommonwealth/raiden/blob/SafeRaiden/raiden/smart_contracts/Token.sol#L23,L29) mechanism. The first method is usual method to transfer/deposit tokens for most users and third party services.

If the user deposits the ERC-20 tokens into the netting channel contract using the `transfer` function, the tokens will get stuck inside your contract without any possibility to recover them. **Which means that it will result in loss of money for Raiden users.**

You can read more about ERC20 critical problems [here](https://medium.com/@dexaran820/erc20-token-standard-critical-problems-3c10fd48657b). [Here](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Feh5sP6oQL1-1NHi-X1dbgT3ch2WdhbXRevDN681Jv4/edit) you can find a small presentation as well.

Each contract that does not allow to extract ERC20 tokens will be a potential token trap. This problem already results in loss of more than $400,000. ([Read this](https://www.reddit.com/r/ethereum/comments/6e8y9o/the_ens_contract_becomes_token_holder_erc20/) or [this](https://www.reddit.com/r/ethereum/comments/6uckdx/reminder_about_problems_of_most_erc20_tokens/) for example).

Again, relying on ERC20 token `transferFrom` deposit mechanism is guaranteed to lead to a loss of money for your users.

## Solution

You **must** reject accidental token `transfer`s by analogy with how the contract rejects accidental Ether transfers. Unfortunately, you can't because of ERC20 token suffers one more critical problem: lack of [event handling](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Event_(computing)) mechanism (transaction is event in this case).

There are two ways to avoid the described problem:

1. Implement a special function that will allow a centralized trusted party to extract any stuck ERC20 token from netting channel by default. In this case, you will likely act as trusted party and you should refund all the users manually. 

https://github.com/EthereumCommonwealth/raiden/blob/SafeRaiden/raiden/smart_contracts/NettingChannelLibrary.sol#L434-L438

2. Do not work with ERC20 tokens at all. Personally I think that relying on mechanism that is guaranteed to result in loss of funds for your users is not a good idea.

issue_title: Stacked inline ignores subclass fields
issue_body:
The following code defines `BigModel` and related polymorphic `SomeModel` with subclass `Spec1`. In admin I added `SomeModel` as `StackedPolymorphicInline` to `BigModel` admin. Problem is, you can create `Spec1` inline and set `number` field but you can't edit or see it after it's saved.

### models.py
```from django.db import models
from polymorphic.models import PolymorphicModel


class BigModel(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=150, null=False, blank=False)


class SomeModel(PolymorphicModel):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=150, null=False, blank=False)
    big = models.ForeignKey(
        BigModel, null=False, blank=False, related_name="somes", 
        on_delete=models.CASCADE)


class Spec1(SomeModel):
    number = models.IntegerField(null=False, blank=False)
```

### admin.py
```from django.contrib import admin
from polymorphic.admin import (
    PolymorphicInlineSupportMixin, StackedPolymorphicInline)
from stacked.models import *

class SomeInline(StackedPolymorphicInline):
    class Spec1Inline(StackedPolymorphicInline.Child):
        model = Spec1

    model = SomeModel
    child_inlines = (Spec1Inline,)


@admin.register(BigModel)
class BigModelAdmin(PolymorphicInlineSupportMixin, admin.ModelAdmin):
    model = BigModel
    inlines = (SomeInline,)
```

Probable issue for missing default value in `number` field is in `formset/models.py` `defaults["instance"]` appears to always be base class.

Am I doing something wrong or is this supposed to be like that? Appreciate any help.

issue_title: Make from_host synchronization part of task specification.
issue_body:
One of the problems with our `exit-and-resume` approach is that it is unclear who should synchronize results back from the server. 

More specifically, currently a task is prepared in a server-neutral manner. That is to say, when a task is translated, it can be either executed as

```
sos execute task_id -q host
```

or

```
sos execute task_id
```
on the host itself.

When the task is submitted and the main sos process quits, the task is still executed on the remote server. When SoS resumes and detects that the task is completed, it will sync the results back and continue. That is to say, **sos has to be re-run with the same `-q` option to complete the tasks**.

I am therefore wondering if we can make the retrieval of results easier so that the results would be available locally when the remote tasks are completed. This does not sound feasible because even if we code the `from_host` information in the job file, the remote host might not be able to copy the file to desktop without IP address and public key authentication.

issue_title: Convert error
sos status --html or sos console
issue_body:
Current `sos status --html` is used by Jupyter `%taskinfo` to display task information. It would be nice to expand this option to display information directly in browser, especially for multiple tasks. This however goes to the task console business and we should perhaps design a dedicated interface for it.

That will be a lot of JS and we should wrap it as a dedicated project similar to what rq and celery are doing.

issue_title: A skip magic?
issue_body:
Say a cell of a notebook needs hours to execute and does not need to be executed each time. It would make sense to use magic 

```
%skip
```

to skip the cell. We can also make it more flexible by something like

```
%skip --if ${os.path.isfile('result.txt'}
```

of course the syntax could be made cleaner.

issue_title: explicit dependency?
issue_body:
Something like,

```
[some_step]

[step]
depends: sos_step('some_step')
```

In this way we can force the execution of `some_step` even if that step does not produce any visible file or target.

A lot of the canvas api accepts query parameters,

example:

This example fetches only students in a course and I have tested it to be working.

	canvas = Canvas(API_URL, API_KEY)

	# First, retrieve the Course object

	'''You can make your own custom request like this'''
	from canvasapi.paginated_list import PaginatedList

issue_title: `new` command should work with URLs
issue_body:
We should be able to run a command like `pulumi new https://github.com/pulumi/examples/cool-kubernetes`, pointing at some directory with a `Pulumi.yaml`, and install it on a cluster. Initial thoughts are that this would work something like:

1. Get the code at that URL, dump to disk in CWD.
1. Inspect the directory for a `Pulumi.yaml`. Retrieve the `quickstart` member from the YAML object, which gives the engine the information (_e.g._, perhaps simple parameters like name, region, _etc_.) it needs to run `update` and provision the resources.
1. Run `update`.

Possibly there could be a separate command, `install`, which skips step (1) and simply runs `update` on the app.

This additionally would allow us to implement something like the "run on Heroku" badge -- "run with Pulumi" would dump the relevant `pulumi new` command into the clipboard, say, and then the user need only run that on the console, and it should all "just work" to provision those resources.

issue_title: Python3.5 support?
issue_body: Is ray supported for Python 3.5 or later?  Official Ray instructions ask to install pssh which is only supported for 2.7, 3.2. Default Ubuntu AMI comes with Python 3.5 . PS, I use paramiko which is 3.5 compatible

issue_title: Make "pip install ray" work.
issue_body:
This should be doable pretty soon, since we've been making progress on building wheels in #600 and #601.

Is there a good way of testing that the wheels all work? Other than installing a bunch of different Python versions on a bunch of different platforms and trying them all out?

Before uploading the wheels, we should make sure that the `runtest.py` can be run and that the `start_ray.sh` script can be used without cloning the repository.

issue_title: Spurious error message about killed worker when script exits
issue_body:
I sometimes see the following message when a script exits (despite the fact that the script runs to completion without any issues).

```
A worker died or was killed while executing a task.

You can inspect errors by running

    ray.error_info()

If this driver is hanging, start a new one with

    ray.init(redis_address="127.0.0.1:25182")
```

I think the problem is that the script, e.g.,

```python
@ray.remote
def f():
  return 1

ray.get([f.remote() for _ in range(10)])
```

is returning as soon as the object is put in the object store, which may happen before the local scheduler realizes that the task has finished, so the local scheduler will still push an error message about killing a worker that is running a task.

One simple fix would be to suppress this message during cleanup.

issue_title: Extract parameters from path doesn't support some Pyramid routes
issue_body:
We don't support patterned paths (that have regex on their attributes e.g. `/path/{foo:[a-z]{8}}`) neither subpath perfixes or sufixes (e.g. `/{*subpath}/path`).

Some opinions on this:
- Simple regex may not be enough to reproduce all the possible path patterns from Pyramid, we may have to analyze some syntax.
- We can consider subpaths simple string parameters or try to reproduce their matching on other declared views. Probably the first approach is easier and straightforward, but the second may look better on client generators and as documentation.

issue_title: Feature Request:  Support for AuthFlow = USERNAME_PASSWORD_AUTH in addition to USER_SRP_AUTH
issue_body:
Hi, I have my userpool configured to use email as the username.  However, when I try to authenticate users with Warrant, I can only do so successfully if I use the guid username.  I get an error using the email address.

I opened a support ticket with AWS and the working sample they sent me for email / password auth uses the USERNAME_PASSWORD_AUTH flow instead of the USER_SRP_AUTH flow that you have built in.

Is there a way you can support both of these modes?  I would think it's pretty common for a system to set up the userpool to use email address as the username because it guarantees uniqueness without being a cryptic, non-memorable value.

In the meantime I'll probably just have to circumvent warrant for this function and use raw boto3.

issue_title: Remove file magic based check in sequence validation
issue_body:
We currently still use magic based file type check in the sequence validation tool (eg. DSM, DCDM). We should remove those check as they are not reliable and can produce different results across system and versions of the file utility.

A more useful test would be to actually decode the image with Pillow / OpenImageIO / ... and check it's type, we then can rely on the fact that all images should be of the same size to assume every others ones have the same type.

issue_title: `datacube dataset add/update` should revert failed attempt
issue_body:
### Expected behaviour

When `datacube dataset add` fails to add new dataset to the DB for whatever reason it should leave database in the original state. 

### Actual behaviour

Lineage data might be added to the index even when top level dataset is rejected for whatever reason. Same goes for update.

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

Note this is just from reading the code, not from running an experiment. 

Example: indexing dataset `good_b` derived from `bad`, derived from `good_a` 

`good_b->bad->good_a`

`dataset add` performs a depth first traversal of the lineage tree, so it will successfully add `good_a` first, then will break out when it will see `bad`. So you end up with updated database even though top-level task have failed.

I don't see any transaction setup/reversal in case of an error, only see transaction for updating dataset locations.

issue_title: Write a helper library for services/daemons
issue_body:
A very common use case for a library like trio is writing long-running server processes. We should make it easy to get this right. This should probably be as a new package on top of trio. Some brainstorming:

Integrating with platform-specific service management systems:

* Windows services

* Launchd integration, in particular socket activation. This is done by calling `launch_activate_socket("lookup-key", ...)`, which returns an array of fds. (Could be multiple e.g. one for IPv4 and one for IPv6).

* Systemd integration: socket activation (depends on #251), watchdog support, `sd_notify`, maybe `sd_pid_notify_with_fds`, ... ([sd-daemon.h](https://github.com/systemd/systemd/blob/master/src/systemd/sd-daemon.h))... these can be done via a binding to libsystemd or through our own implementation (they're all pretty trivially defined in terms of environment variable manipulation etc.), and either way there's a choice whether to write our own or use an existing one.

It might also make sense to support classic double-fork daemonization, though that's fallen out of popularity these days.

Generic services:

* abstracting away the differences between different socket activation styles (a bit tricky, since launchd always uses names but we can't really introspect the returned sockets – again see #251 – while systemd prefers to use introspection and only supports names as a fallback.)

* graceful shutdown -- SIGTERM, however windows services do it

issue_title: ProbabilitySampler does not work with nested spans
issue_body:
## Description
When `ProbabilitySampler` decides not to sample, the following fails with `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'span'`:

```
with tracer.span(name='span1') as span1:
    do_something_to_trace()
    with span1.span(name='span1_child1') as span1_child1:
        do_something_to_trace()
```

Of course, since if the sampler decides not to sample, then the tracer will be replaced with `NoopTracer` here: https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/cfcfda584ba984e6f0e89684120c1ea94271b2c9/opencensus/trace/tracer.py#L88 . As a consequence, spans are managed by `NullContextManager`s: https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/ad75bf58fc297eeb2979e3973c09907772cb7acc/opencensus/trace/tracers/noop_tracer.py#L48. When that `ContextManager` is entered, nothing is bound to the `with` expression because `__enter__` does not return (returns `None`) here: https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/cfcfda584ba984e6f0e89684120c1ea94271b2c9/opencensus/trace/tracers/base.py#L74 . 

So this is why in the example `span1` is `None` if the sampler decides not to sample. 

## Environment
Python version: 3.5.1 (on CentOS 7 based Docker image)

pip freeze:
cachetools==2.0.1
certifi==2018.1.18
chardet==3.0.4
dill==0.2.7.1
future==0.16.0
google-api-core==0.1.4
google-auth==1.4.1
google-cloud-core==0.28.1
google-cloud-trace==0.17.0
google-gax==0.15.16
googleapis-common-protos==1.5.3
grpcio==1.11.0
grpcio-tools==1.11.0
idna==2.6
opencensus==0.1.4
ply==3.8
protobuf==3.5.2.post1
pyasn1==0.4.2
pyasn1-modules==0.2.1
pytz==2018.4
requests==2.18.4
rsa==3.4.2
six==1.11.0
urllib3==1.22

## Proposal
I am really not that familiar with the code, but I would probably let the exporters know about the sampling decision in `SpanData`: https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/trace/span_data.py#L19 and move any logic that depends on the sampling decision into the base exporter. Not sure if this is the best approach though. Another way would be just to let `NullContextManager` bind to `Span` objects that implement the same interface, but they may have a more specific type that indicates they are not to be reported -- then the type check could be done in https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/trace/tracers/context_tracer.py#L99 . It's not trivial, but surely solvable. 

Please do let me know if I missed anything or if you need help fixing this.

issue_title: There needs to be a better way of clearing out context from test results
Error on CircleCI due to high user ids within the image files
issue_body:
Copied from here: https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/localstack/issues/54/trouble-using-docker-image-on-circleci-due

---

Hi guys,
Last week I tried to have localstack running within my CI build in CircleCI and faced various problems. I got in touch with them and their engineers investigated the issue. Here's what they found out:
Looking at this particular container, it has such high uid that we will not be able to reasonably accomodate them while keeping user namespace effectively:

```
notnoop@placeholder:~$ sudo docker run -it --rm --entrypoint '' atlassianlabs/localstack:0.5.5 ls -lha /opt/code/localstack/localstack/dashboard/web/node_modules/comment-parser
total 92
drwxr-xr-x    3 17027476 19314187    4.0K Jun 28 10:02 .
drwxr-xr-x  933 root     root       36.0K Jun 28 10:02 ..
-rw-r--r--    1 8248842  21495225     187 Jan 26  2015 .jshintrc
-rw-r--r--    1 8248842  52370573      97 Mar  9  2014 .npmignore
-rw-r--r--    1 40347806 36259175      91 Nov  7  2015 .travis.yml
-rw-r--r--    1 15014210 18816141    1.1K Nov 25  2015 CHANGELOG.md
-rw-r--r--    1 21557324 94156302    1.1K Mar  9  2014 LICENSE
-rw-r--r--    1 42864389 2038925     4.7K Nov 25  2015 README.md
-rw-r--r--    1 8248842  2038925     1.2K Nov 25  2015 index.js
-rw-r--r--    1 17027476 19314187    1.8K Jun 28 10:02 package.json
-rw-r--r--    1 61222861 63957313    8.5K Nov 25  2015 parser.js
drwxr-xr-x    3 10819906 22837403    4.0K Jun 28 10:02 tests
```

The default limit is 65536 but README.md in this directory used 42864389 . This is probably as misconfiguration on the docker image creation part and we/user can consider contributing to image to avoid this.
Since a lot of people might use localstack within CircleCI, I figured it might be good to fix this to improve localstack adoption.
Thanks.

issue_title: Add support for GET /merge_requests call
issue_body:
## Description of the problem, including code/CLI snippet

Since GitLab 9.5 there is a `GET /merge_requests` call that returns all the merge requests the current authenticated user have access to.

This call is very useful when doing actions over all the merge requests, as is much more efficient than doing `GET /projects/:id/merge_requests` for each project.

A good place for its manager to be would be in the `mergerequests` property in the client object, the same name used in project objects.

  - python-gitlab version: 1.5.1
  - API version you are using (v3/v4): v4
  - Gitlab server version (or gitlab.com): 9.5 and greater.
  - API call URL doc: https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/api/merge_requests.html#list-merge-requests

issue_title: Create, human readable, documentation from solidity smart contracts
Support implicit type conversions in from evaluation returned values
Build versioning and updates
issue_body:
Users need to be able to update to new versions of blurr once they're available.

Ensuring a smooth update is a very tricky task. In order to minimize the hassle we'll only guarantee updates between contiguous minor versions. This means:

- `1.1 --> 1.2` is a valid update
- `1.1 --> 1.3` is **not** a valid update. Users need to update step by step: `1.1 --> 1.2 --> 1.3`

issue_title: Add support for Fashion-MNIST dataset
issue_body:
[fashion-mnist: A MNIST-like fashion product database.](https://github.com/zalandoresearch/fashion-mnist)
Here is the reasons:
* **MNIST is too easy.** Convolutional nets can achieve 99.7% on MNIST. Classic machine learning algorithms can also achieve 97% easily. 
* **MNIST is overused.** In this April 2017 Twitter thread, Google Brain research scientist and deep learning expert Ian Goodfellow calls for people to move away from MNIST.
* **MNIST can not represent modern CV tasks**, as noted in this April 2017 Twitter thread, deep learning expert/Keras author François Chollet.
* **Many ML libraries already include Fashion-MNIST data/API**, such as, PyTorch, Torch, MXNet etc.
* **It shares the same image size and structure of training and testing splits with MNIST**

issue_title: Issues with the Python 3 grammar
issue_body:
Hello,

I am having difficulties with the Python 3 parser example. It seems that any code with an indent breaks, for example, as in:

"""
def a(x):
    pass
"""

If parses the first line well, after which I get "no token for "p" (from "pass"). Similar results occur with "return" (no token for "r"), arithmetic expressions (e.g., "x*3"; no token for "x"), or anything else within the function definition, for that matter. Similar problems arise when defining classes as well. I was using the lalr parser with the contextual lexer.

If you have any general pointers as to what could be the mistake (e.g., the grammar, lark itself, or me) I would be happy to try to submit a pull request! (In case you are curious, I am using Lark as the backend for a new tool called Lassi, which will allow people to code Python with keywords in their own natural language, before transpiling behind the scenes to regular Python keywords right before execution.)

Many thanks,
Julien Malard

issue_title: Mapping types seem to be misrepresented
issue_body: {1: 1, a: a} yields { '1': 1, a: 'a' } instead of { 1: 1, 'a': 'a' }
